CveFindings.Name: Apache Tomcat - Digest authenticator will authenticate any unknown user
CveFindings.Description:
[CVE-2026-43512](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h6fc-48rj-7qqh): Apache Tomcat - Digest authenticator will authenticate any unknown user

Severity: CRITICAL

Affected dependencies:
  - org.apache.tomcat.embed:tomcat-embed-core:10.1.26 (declared at pom.xml:55) → patch: 9.0.118

Recommended fix:
  - Upgrade org.apache.tomcat.embed:tomcat-embed-core to 9.0.118 or later
CveFindings.Explanation:

CveFindings.Name: Sandboxed Thymeleaf expressions vulnerable to improper recognition of unauthorized syntax patterns
CveFindings.Description:
[CVE-2026-41901](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-c9ph-gxww-7744): Sandboxed Thymeleaf expressions vulnerable to improper recognition of unauthorized syntax patterns

Severity: CRITICAL

Affected dependencies:
  - org.thymeleaf:thymeleaf:3.1.2.RELEASE (declared at pom.xml:63) → patch: 3.1.5.RELEASE
  - org.thymeleaf:thymeleaf-spring6:3.1.2.RELEASE (declared at pom.xml:63) → patch: 3.1.5.RELEASE

Recommended fix:
  - Upgrade org.thymeleaf:thymeleaf to 3.1.5.RELEASE or later
  - Upgrade org.thymeleaf:thymeleaf-spring6 to 3.1.5.RELEASE or later

[CVE-2026-41901](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-c9ph-gxww-7744): Sandboxed Thymeleaf expressions vulnerable to improper recognition of unauthorized syntax patterns

CweFindings.Name: Insufficient Logging
CweFindings.Description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.
CweFindings.Explanation: The application controllers (OwnerController, PetController, VisitController) perform data-modifying operations (create, update) without any security event logging. No logger is declared or used in any of the main application controllers or service classes. Security-critical operations such as creating or updating owner/pet records, and adding visits are not logged, making audit trails and security incident detection impossible.
